ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED ON 1/4/2011 ADOPTED: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1-4 AND 6-11 (SOPHISTICATED INTERMEDIARY AND BULK SUPPLIER DEFENSES, LOSS OF CONSORTIUM AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 5 (FALSE REPRESENTATION). THE SOPHISTICATED INTERMEDIARY DEFENSE DOES NOT SHIELD DEFENDANT FROM LIABILITY. (STEWART). DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SHOWING WHAT KNOWLEDGE HAMILTON MATERIALS HAD ABOUT THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS AND WHAT WARNINGS, IF ANY, IT PROVIDED TO HAMILTON MATERIALS. THE BULK SUPPLIER DEFENSE IS NOT AVAILABLE TO A SUPPLIER OF RAW ASBESTOS IN ASBESTOS EXPOSURE CASES BECAUSE ASBESTOS IS INHERENTLY DANGEROUS AND RAW ASBESTOS FIBERS DO NOT CHANGE WHEN THEY BECOME A COMPONENT OF AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT. (GARZA; JENKINS; ARENA). AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT MADE MISREPRESENTATIONS ABOUT AND/OR WITHHELD MATERIAL INFORMATION CONCERNING ITS KNOWLEDGE OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS AND THUS WHETHER IT ACTED IN CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE SAFETY OF PERSONS WHO WERE EXPOSED TO ITS ASBESTOS. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED HER CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling issued on 1/4/2011 adopted: Defendant Union Carbide's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-11 (sophisticated intermediary and bulk supplier defenses, loss of consortium and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff dismissed her cause of action for false representation. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
